utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present and split into physical and mental with plan names and explicit monthly costs. Fully specified with costs. 2) property_coverage: Present but specifies only equipment_insurance; the required item is home/contents insurance with costs. It lacks home/contents scope and is not aligned, though it has a cost. Not fully specified per requirement. 3) travel_coverage: Present with plan name and monthly cost. Fully specified with costs. 4) additional_services: Present with listed services but no costs; requirement says any enhancement services with costs. Missing costs, so not fully specified. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly and annual cost breakdown and details. Fully specified. Thus, fully specified items: health_coverage, travel_coverage, total_budget = 3 out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each item in the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding item name in the ground-truth ratings and return the numeric rating. The final plan includes: (1) physical health: 'InsurePlus Standard Health' which corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 10; (2) mental health: 'InsurePlus Standard Mental Health' corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage.mental_health with rating 7; (3) equipment insurance: 'PrimeHome Premium Equipment Coverage' should match 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' under property_protection.home_content with rating 3; (4) adventure travel: 'InsurePlus Standard Travel' corresponds to 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 10. The additional services and budget lines are not rated items in the ground truth.
